title: salix sericea var. subsericea
intro: Salix sericea var. subsericea, also known as the Arctic willow, is a variety of the white willow tree that is native to the Arctic tundra regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which are attributed to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The medicinal properties of Salix sericea var. subsericea have been found to provide relief from pain, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to soothe sore muscles, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing.
